title: Egyptian Koshari
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The lentils, rice, macaroni, chickpeas, onions, tomato sauce, vinegar-garlic sauce, olive oil, herbs, spices, and seasonings contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the dish is built around lentils and chickpeas, with legumes, grains, tomato, aromatics, spices, and acid creating a filling main dish without cholesterol-containing ingredients. The structure supports LDL lowering through a legume-forward base and uses technique, crisp onions, and sharp sauces for satisfaction rather than saturated fat.
intro: Koshari is one of Egypt's great street-food meals: rice, lentils, macaroni, chickpeas, tomato sauce, vinegar-garlic sauce, and crisp onions stacked into a bowl that is cheap, filling, loud, and deeply satisfying. Its power comes from contrast. The grains and legumes are hearty, the tomato sauce is warm and spiced, the vinegar-garlic sauce cuts through everything, and the onions bring crunch. It is comfort food built from pantry staples, and it fits naturally into a cholesterol-free kitchen when the textures are handled carefully.

items.prep: rinsed until the water runs mostly clear

stepGroups.title: Cook the Lentils
items: Combine the lentils ({{quantity:brown-lentils}}), water ({{quantity:water-lentils}}), and bay leaf ({{quantity:bay-leaf}}) in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Cook for 18 to 22 minutes, until the lentils are tender but still hold their shape.
Drain well and discard the bay leaf.
Spread the lentils on a plate or small tray so they cool slightly and stop cooking.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Rice
items: Combine the rinsed rice ({{quantity:long-grain-rice}}), olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-rice}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-rice}}), and water ({{quantity:water-rice}}) in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the rice stand, covered, for 10 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and keep covered.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Macaroni and Warm the Chickpeas
items: Bring a pot of water to a boil and season it with the fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-pasta-water}}).
Cook the macaroni ({{quantity:elbow-macaroni}}) until just tender.
Drain the macaroni and keep it warm.
Warm the chickpeas ({{quantity:cooked-chickpeas}}) in a small pan with a splash of water, then drain.

stepGroups.title: Make the Crisp Onion Topping
items: Heat the oven to 425°F.
Toss the sliced onions ({{quantity:yellow-onions-topping}}) with the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-onions}}) and fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-onions}}).
Spread the onions on a parchment-lined sheet pan in a thin, even layer.
Bake for 25 to 35 minutes, stirring every 8 to 10 minutes, until the onions are deeply browned, frizzled at the edges, and crisp in spots.
Transfer the onions to a rack or plate so steam does not soften them.

stepGroups.title: Build the Tomato Sauce
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-sauce}}) in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion ({{quantity:yellow-onion-sauce}}) and cook for 5 to 6 minutes, until softened.

Add the garlic ({{quantity:garlic-sauce}}), cumin ({{quantity:ground-cumin-sauce}}), coriander ({{quantity:ground-coriander-sauce}}), smoked paprika ({{quantity:smoked-paprika-sauce}}), and cayenne ({{quantity:cayenne-sauce}}).
Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until the spices smell toasted.
Add the tomato paste ({{quantity:tomato-paste}}) and cook for 2 minutes, stirring often, until it darkens slightly.
Add the crushed tomatoes ({{quantity:crushed-tomatoes}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-sauce}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper-sauce}}).
Simmer for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and tastes cooked rather than raw.
Turn off the heat and stir in the red wine vinegar ({{quantity:red-wine-vinegar-sauce}}).

stepGroups.title: Make the Vinegar-Garlic Sauce
items: Stir together the red wine vinegar ({{quantity:red-wine-vinegar-garlic-sauce}}), water ({{quantity:water-garlic-sauce}}), garlic ({{quantity:garlic-vinegar-sauce}}), cumin ({{quantity:ground-cumin-vinegar-sauce}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-vinegar-sauce}}), and cayenne ({{quantity:cayenne-vinegar-sauce}}).
Let the sauce stand for at least 10 minutes so the garlic sharpness softens slightly.

stepGroups.title: Assemble and Serve
items: For each serving, layer rice, lentils, macaroni, and chickpeas in a bowl.
Spoon tomato sauce over the top.
Drizzle with a small amount of vinegar-garlic sauce.
Finish with crisp onions.
Serve immediately, with extra tomato sauce and vinegar-garlic sauce at the table.
